metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add Approach descriptions + ACs for 'finding decisions / false-positive proxy' and 'first-pass verification' metrics, or remove them from the 'What' section to match the actual scope covered by Tests and ACs.
evidence: What: 'finding decisions / false-positive proxy, first-pass verification'
Approach only covers: 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes; env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS'
Tests section: only two scenarios described, neither covering finding decisions or first-pass verification
ACs: only two checklist items, neither covering finding decisions or first-pass verification
findings.finding: 'What' lists 'finding decisions / false-positive proxy' and 'first-pass verification' as deliverables, but neither the Approach, Tests, nor Acceptance Criteria sections contain any description or coverage of these metrics — they are orphaned deliverables with no implementation plan and no verifying criterion.
findings.location: ## What (lines 3-4) vs ## Approach, ## Tests, ## Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: An implementer reads 'What' and believes they must implement false-positive proxy and first-pass verification metrics, but finds no specification of how to compute them, no test scaffold, and no AC to verify completion — work scope is undefined.
A reviewer checking ACs at delivery cannot verify whether false-positive proxy or first-pass verification were correctly implemented because no criterion exists.
findings.suggested_fix: Option A (scope-match): Remove 'finding decisions / false-positive proxy, first-pass verification' from the What section so the plan scope matches its ACs. Option B (full coverage): For each metric, add a sentence to Approach describing the derivation formula, a test case in Tests, and a corresponding AC with an exact expected value.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and clarify which sidecar(s) supply `llm_calls` for cost-per-accepted-change: confirm that `COMPLETION_VERDICT` payloads do NOT carry a `metrics` key (read `completion_sidecar.py:build_payload`), and either scope the metric to `REVIEW_RESULT`-only or add a prerequisite task to extend `COMPLETION_VERDICT` to include the metrics field.
evidence: Plan: 'The sidecars already persist per-run metrics{llm_calls,det_ms,llm_ms,total_ms}'
completion_sidecar.py build_payload() PASS branch returns: schema, verdict, ticket_id, criteria, findings, runner, model, material_fingerprint — no 'metrics' key
completion_sidecar.py build_payload() FAIL branch returns: schema, verdict, ticket_id, findings, remediation, certifiable, runner, model, material_fingerprint — no 'metrics' key
By contrast, REVIEW_RESULT sidecar build_payload (plan_review/sidecar.py:497) explicitly lifts coverage['metrics'] into the payload
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'The sidecars already persist per-run metrics{llm_calls,det_ms,llm_ms,total_ms}' for both REVIEW_RESULT and COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecars, but the COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecar's build_payload() in completion_sidecar.py stores no 'metrics' key in either the PASS (SCHEMA_PASS) or FAIL (SCHEMA) payload — only findings/criteria/remediation/certifiable/runner/model/material_fingerprint. The llm_calls cost-proxy is absent from COMPLETION_VERDICT records.
findings.location: Approach section: 'The sidecars already persist per-run metrics{llm_calls,det_ms,llm_ms,total_ms}'; src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py build_payload()
scenarios: If cost-per-accepted-change is computed over completion-gate PASS records, llm_calls will always be missing/None from COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecars, producing incorrect (zero or null) cost metrics
If the new sidecar_metrics.py reader assumes llm_calls is present in all sidecar types, it will silently produce wrong results for completion-gate data
findings.suggested_fix: Scope the plan's cost-proxy metrics (llm_calls, det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms) explicitly to REVIEW_RESULT sidecars only, since COMPLETION_VERDICT build_payload() does not lift coverage['metrics']. If completion-gate cost coverage is needed, add a prerequisite to amend completion_sidecar.py:build_payload() to include the metrics field.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'accepted (PASS) closes' means plan-review gate PASSes (REVIEW_RESULT) or actual ticket closes (COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS at transition_close); if the latter, specify that the denominator reads COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS records, not REVIEW_RESULT PASSes.
evidence: build_payload in plan_review/sidecar.py stamps 'verdict': verdict.get('verdict') — this is the plan-review gate verdict (PASS/BLOCK), not a close signal
transition_close.py emits COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS at close time (line 258: completion_sidecar.emit(result, ...)) — this is the actual 'accepted close' event
A ticket can have a REVIEW_RESULT PASS (gate passed) but never be closed; counting those as 'accepted changes' overstates denominator
findings.finding: The plan conflates 'accepted (PASS) close' with a plan-review REVIEW_RESULT PASS verdict. In the codebase, a plan-review PASS is a gate pass — the ticket may still be in-flight. An 'accepted change' (closed ticket) maps to a COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS (emitted at transition_close). Summing llm_calls over REVIEW_RESULT PASSes and calling the denominator 'accepted closes' measures the wrong thing: it counts gate passes, not completed work items, so the metric will be systematically inflated by tickets that pass review but are never closed.
findings.location: Approach section — 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes'
scenarios: A ticket passes plan review multiple times (re-reviewed after edits) then is abandoned — each REVIEW_RESULT PASS inflates the denominator, making cost-per-accepted-change appear artificially low
A high-churn ticket with many plan-review PASSes before a single close skews the metric
findings.suggested_fix: Define the denominator as COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS records (schema='completion_verifier_pass_v1'), which are emitted at transition_close and represent actual accepted closes. Sum llm_calls from all REVIEW_RESULT sidecar events for the same ticket (across all gate passes in the remediation loop) and divide by 1 for each COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Approach which sidecar(s) supply `llm_calls` for the cost-per-accepted-change numerator — confirm it is the `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars for all gate attempts on the ticket, not the `COMPLETION_VERDICT` sidecar (which carries no `metrics` block).
evidence: completion_sidecar.build_payload() for PASS returns {schema: completion_verifier_pass_v1, verdict, ticket_id, criteria, findings, runner, model, material_fingerprint} — no metrics key
plan_review/sidecar.build_payload() attaches metrics: (verdict.get('coverage', {}) or {}).get('metrics', {}) — present for plan-review runs
Plan says 'sidecars already persist per-run metrics{llm_calls,...}' without disambiguating which sidecar type carries this field
findings.finding: The plan asserts cost-per-accepted-change uses `llm_calls` summed across attempts, but `COMPLETION_VERDICT` PASS records (schema `completion_verifier_pass_v1`) carry no `metrics` block at all — only `schema/verdict/ticket_id/criteria/findings/runner/model/material_fingerprint`. If 'accepted (PASS) close' means a completion PASS, the llm_calls signal needed for the numerator comes only from the plan-review `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar, not the completion sidecar — an implicit assumption the plan never states.
findings.location: Approach section ('cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes') / src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py:build_payload()
scenarios: sidecar_metrics.py reads llm_calls from COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS records and gets 0/None for every accepted close, producing a cost-per-accepted-change of 0 or a division error
Metric silently sources llm_calls only from REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (correct behavior) but the plan gives no basis to verify this is intended

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `llm_calls` is only available in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (not COMPLETION_VERDICT), and either scope the cost-per-accepted-change metric exclusively to REVIEW_RESULT or explicitly extend the COMPLETION_VERDICT payload before reading it.
evidence: Plan: 'The sidecars already persist per-run metrics{llm_calls,det_ms,llm_ms,total_ms}'
completion_sidecar.py build_payload() PASS branch returns: schema, verdict, ticket_id, criteria, findings, runner, model, material_fingerprint — no 'metrics' key
completion_sidecar.py build_payload() FAIL branch returns: schema, verdict, ticket_id, findings, remediation, certifiable, runner, model, material_fingerprint — no 'metrics' key
plan_review/sidecar.py line 497: 'metrics': (verdict.get('coverage', {}) or {}).get('metrics', {}) — only REVIEW_RESULT has this
code_review/finalize.py line 186-193: coverage['metrics'] = {..., 'llm_calls': ...} — only code-review REVIEW_RESULT has this
findings.finding: The plan states 'The sidecars already persist per-run metrics{llm_calls,det_ms,llm_ms,total_ms}' as a factual premise for cost-per-accepted-change, but inspection of completion_sidecar.py build_payload() confirms that COMPLETION_VERDICT payloads carry NO 'metrics' key in either the PASS branch (schema=completion_verifier_pass_v1) or the FAIL branch (schema=completion_verifier_fail_v1). Only REVIEW_RESULT sidecars embed coverage['metrics'] (confirmed in plan_review/sidecar.py line 497 and code_review/finalize.py line 186). The llm_calls cost-proxy field is absent from all COMPLETION_VERDICT records.
findings.location: Approach section: 'The sidecars already persist per-run metrics{llm_calls,det_ms,llm_ms,total_ms}'
scenarios: If cost-per-accepted-change is computed using llm_calls from COMPLETION_VERDICT PASSes, the field will be absent (KeyError or None), silently producing zero or crashing
If the metric is scoped to REVIEW_RESULT only, the plan must say so explicitly
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Approach: 'llm_calls is sourced only from REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (plan-review + code-review); COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecars carry no metrics field. cost-per-accepted-change is therefore computed over REVIEW_RESULT PASSes only.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an Acceptance Criterion (or explicitly defer/drop) for each of the three metrics named in 'What' but absent from the ACs: finding decisions / false-positive proxy, first-pass verification, and LLM calls/latency.
evidence: 'What' lists: 'LLM cost proxy (llm_calls, latency), cost-per-accepted-change, finding decisions / false-positive proxy, first-pass verification, and env-diagnosis intervals'
ACs cover only cost-per-accepted-change and env-diagnosis interval; finding decisions, false-positive proxy, first-pass verification, and raw llm_calls/latency metrics have no AC
'each non-deferred goal has a faithful, end-state-observable proof' — E3 checklist item
findings.finding: Three metrics named in 'What' as deliverables — finding decisions / false-positive proxy, first-pass verification, and LLM calls/latency — have no corresponding Acceptance Criterion and no end-state-observable proof of delivery. They are not marked deferred, so they are non-deferred goals without verifiable completion.
findings.location: ## What / ## Acceptance Criteria
scenarios: An implementer who reads only the ACs ships a module that computes exactly two metrics and passes CI, while the three other promised metrics are silently absent — the plan's 'What' is not delivered.
findings.suggested_fix: Either add one AC per missing metric (e.g., 'a fixture with two PASS and one REJECT verdict returns a false-positive proxy of 0.33') or move them to an explicit 'Out of scope / future' section so implementers know they are intentionally deferred.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test cases (and specify the expected return value) for: (a) cost-per-accepted-change when PASS count is zero (should return None, 0, or raise a documented exception — not ZeroDivisionError uncaught), and (b) env-diagnosis interval when an ERROR row has no subsequent PASS on the same ticket (should return None or be omitted from results).
evidence: 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes' — no guard or test for zero accepted closes
'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket' — no test for an ERROR with no following PASS
AC2 fixture has exactly 1 PASS; zero-PASS case is absent
AC1 fixture has a matched ERROR+PASS; unmatched ERROR case is absent
findings.finding: The test plan covers only happy-path metric computations but omits two concrete failure paths: (1) cost-per-accepted-change when there are zero PASS (accepted) closes — dividing summed llm_calls by 0 raises ZeroDivisionError or produces an undefined result; (2) env-diagnosis pairing when an ERROR row has no subsequent PASS on the same ticket — the pairing logic's behavior is unspecified and untested.
findings.location: Tests section and Acceptance Criteria — both ACs
scenarios: A ticket that was opened and closed as ERROR only (e.g. env never recovered) triggers division-by-zero in cost-per-accepted-change
A metrics query over a window where no change was accepted returns an error instead of 0 or None
An env-diagnosis query over a ticket whose ERROR was never resolved returns an unhandled None/exception
findings.suggested_fix: Add two fixture-based unit tests: `test_cost_per_accepted_change_no_accepted` asserting the function returns `None` (or a sentinel) rather than raising when the denominator is zero; and `test_env_diagnosis_unmatched_error` asserting that an ERROR with no following PASS is excluded from (or reported as open-ended in) the interval results. Document the chosen contract in the function docstring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies the registry registration, e.g., assert that importing the registry and calling its listing/lookup function returns the new sidecar metrics by name.
evidence: ABSENCE: neither AC references registry.py or any observable effect of registration (e.g., the metric appearing in a registry listing).
findings.finding: The Files section lists 'registered in src/rebar/metrics/registry.py' as a deliverable, but no AC verifies the registration was performed — the registry step can be silently omitted and both ACs still pass.
findings.location: Files section — 'src/rebar/metrics/sidecar_metrics.py — new: reader over the sidecars, registered in src/rebar/metrics/registry.py'
scenarios: An executor implements and tests sidecar_metrics.py but forgets to register it; both ACs pass and the deliverable is signed off, but the metric is unreachable by the registry consumers.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`python -c "from rebar.metrics.registry import get_metrics; names = [m.name for m in get_metrics()]; assert \'cost_per_accepted_change\' in names and \'env_diagnosis_interval\' in names"` exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended behavior when an ERROR has no subsequent PASS on the same ticket (e.g., 'skip the pair and exclude from the interval list', 'return None for that entry', or 'raise ValueError').
evidence: 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket' — no mention of the no-subsequent-PASS case
findings.finding: The plan does not specify what the implementation should return or do when an ERROR sidecar has no subsequent PASS on the same ticket — the executor must guess (skip/None/raise/sentinel).
findings.location: Approach section — 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket'
scenarios: An ERROR that is the last event on a ticket (never resolved) — the pairing logic has no specified behavior, leading to divergent implementations.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Approach: 'If an ERROR has no subsequent PASS on the same ticket, the interval is excluded from results (the entry is skipped).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the zero-denominator default for cost-per-accepted-change (e.g., 'return None when there are no PASS closes').
evidence: 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes' — no zero-denominator handling specified
findings.finding: The plan does not specify behavior when there are zero PASS (accepted) closes, making the cost-per-accepted-change formula division-by-zero undefined — the executor must choose a default.
findings.location: Approach section — 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes'
scenarios: A fixture with 3 attempts all resulting in FAIL — the metric has no specified return value (None, 0, float('inf'), raise).
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Approach: 'When there are no accepted (PASS) closes, cost-per-accepted-change returns None.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define exactly which field+value(s) classify a close as 'accepted' for each sidecar stream (e.g., 'for COMPLETION_VERDICT: schema=="completion_verifier_pass_v1"; for REVIEW_RESULT: verdict=="PASS"') and whether both streams contribute to the denominator.
evidence: Plan phrase: 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes'
completion_sidecar.py SCHEMA='completion_verifier_fail_v1', SCHEMA_PASS='completion_verifier_pass_v1' — PASS/FAIL distinguished by schema tag, not verdict field alone
plan_review_verdict.schema.json: verdict is 'PASS | BLOCK | INDETERMINATE (normalized by the orchestrator; not a strict enum)'
No definition of 'accepted close' rule co-located with the schema description in the plan
findings.finding: The plan's 'accepted (PASS) closes' term never defines which field and value identify a close as 'accepted': COMPLETION_VERDICT uses schema-tagged routing (completion_verifier_pass_v1 for PASS, completion_verifier_fail_v1 for FAIL), while REVIEW_RESULT uses a plain verdict string (PASS/BLOCK/INDETERMINATE). An implementer reading multiple sidecar streams cannot determine whether to test verdict=='PASS', schema=='completion_verifier_pass_v1', or both — these are structurally distinct membership tests that produce different result sets.
findings.location: Approach section: 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes'
scenarios: An implementer who tests verdict=='PASS' on COMPLETION_VERDICT FAIL records would get wrong counts (FAIL records also have verdict='FAIL', but the schema field is the canonical classifier)
An implementer who reads only REVIEW_RESULT sidecars would miss completion-gate PASSes and under-count accepted closes

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the fallback for an ERROR that has no subsequent PASS on the same ticket (e.g., 'exclude from the interval list', 'yield None for that entry', or 'leave the interval open until a PASS arrives').
evidence: Plan: 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket' — no mention of the case where no subsequent PASS exists
ABSENCE: no 'if no PASS follows, then...' clause anywhere in the plan
AC only tests the happy-path: 'an ERROR row paired with a later PASS on the same ticket yields a diagnosis-interval duration equal to the fixture timestamp delta'
findings.finding: The plan does not specify the fallback behavior when an ERROR sidecar record has no subsequent PASS on the same ticket. The Approach says 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS' but is silent on what the implementation should do for an unresolved ERROR (the most recent event, or a ticket that closed in FAIL). An implementer must choose between: skip/exclude the entry, return None/sentinel, raise ValueError, or leave the interval open-ended.
findings.location: Approach section: 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket'
scenarios: An ERROR is the last sidecar event on a ticket that was later abandoned — pairing logic has no specified behavior
A ticket where every close attempt errored and none passed — the interval list result is undefined
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Approach: 'An ERROR with no subsequent PASS on the same ticket is excluded from the diagnosis-interval list (open intervals are not emitted).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Approach how `sidecar_metrics.py` accesses `gate_error_v1` ERROR records (e.g., raw ticket-dir enumeration with explicit schema=`gate_error_v1` filter, or a new `all_gate_errors()` helper in `gate_error_sidecar.py`), since `all_review_results()` silently drops them.
evidence: plan_review/sidecar.py all_review_results(): `payload.get('schema') in ('plan_review_result_v1', 'plan_review_result_v2')` — gate_error_v1 is skipped
gate_error_sidecar.py: ERROR records tagged schema='gate_error_v1' ride the REVIEW_RESULT stream
Plan: 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket' — implies reading both normal reviews and ERROR records together
findings.finding: The plan states it reads the ERROR outcome from REVIEW_RESULT/COMPLETION_VERDICT sidecars, but the existing `all_review_results()` reader (in `plan_review/sidecar.py`) schema-guards strictly to `plan_review_result_v1`/`plan_review_result_v2` and silently skips `gate_error_v1` records. The new sidecar_metrics.py cannot use `all_review_results()` to see ERROR records; it must either perform raw file enumeration with a separate schema filter or use a new reader — neither of which is named in the plan.
findings.location: Approach — 'the additive ERROR outcome'
scenarios: If sidecar_metrics.py delegates to all_review_results(), ERROR records are invisible and the env-diagnosis interval metric always returns empty — a silent data gap with no test catching it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the ERROR→PASS pairing rule for env-diagnosis: state whether pairing is per-stream (ERROR on REVIEW_RESULT pairs only with the next PASS on REVIEW_RESULT; ERROR on COMPLETION_VERDICT pairs only with the next PASS on COMPLETION_VERDICT) or cross-stream, and confirm whether gate identity (the 'gate' field in gate_error_v1) is used as an additional pairing key.
evidence: gate_error_sidecar.py _GATE_EVENT_TYPE maps plan_review/code_review -> REVIEW_RESULT and completion -> COMPLETION_VERDICT
build_gate_error_payload returns {schema, verdict, gate, error} — no ticket_id in the payload itself
REVIEW_RESULT and COMPLETION_VERDICT are separate file streams in the ticket directory, distinguished by filename suffix
findings.finding: The env-diagnosis ERROR→PASS pairing mechanism is ambiguous about which event stream's PASS to pair with an ERROR. gate_error_v1 records ride on REVIEW_RESULT (plan/code review gates) or COMPLETION_VERDICT (completion gate). The plan says 'pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket' without specifying same-stream pairing. A completion-gate ERROR on ticket X followed by a plan-review-gate PASS on ticket X would be in different streams but could incorrectly form a pair if the reader scans all sidecar files indiscriminately.
findings.location: Approach section — 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket'
scenarios: A completion-gate ERROR (COMPLETION_VERDICT stream) is followed by a plan-review PASS (REVIEW_RESULT stream) — if the reader merges both streams by timestamp, it incorrectly pairs cross-gate records and reports a spuriously short diagnosis interval
A REVIEW_RESULT PASS (plan-review gate) appears before a COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS after the same ERROR, giving different intervals depending on which stream is scanned

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which sidecar stream(s) to scan for ERROR records and for subsequent PASS records in the env-diagnosis pairing (e.g., 'scan the same stream as the ERROR', 'scan all streams on the same ticket', or 'scope to REVIEW_RESULT only').
evidence: gate_error_sidecar.py _GATE_EVENT_TYPE = {'plan_review': 'REVIEW_RESULT', 'code_review': 'REVIEW_RESULT', 'completion': 'COMPLETION_VERDICT'}
gate_error_sidecar.py: 'The record rides the SAME event stream as the gate's normal sidecar — REVIEW_RESULT for plan-review/code-review, COMPLETION_VERDICT for completion'
Plan: 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket' — no stream specification
No decision rule for cross-stream pairing co-located with the schema description
findings.finding: The env-diagnosis pairing rule 'pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket' is structurally ambiguous because ERROR records (schema=gate_error_v1) can ride on EITHER the REVIEW_RESULT stream (for plan/code review) or the COMPLETION_VERDICT stream (for completion), as established by gate_error_sidecar.py's _GATE_EVENT_TYPE map. The plan never specifies: (a) which stream to scan for ERROR records, (b) which stream to look for the subsequent PASS, or (c) whether a cross-stream pairing is valid (e.g., ERROR on COMPLETION_VERDICT paired with PASS on REVIEW_RESULT).
findings.location: Approach section: 'env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket'
scenarios: A ticket that had a completion-gate ERROR (COMPLETION_VERDICT stream) followed by a plan-review PASS (REVIEW_RESULT stream) — should this pair count as a resolved env-diagnosis interval?
An implementer who scans only REVIEW_RESULT for both ERROR and PASS would miss completion-gate ERRORs

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a guard (return `None`, `0`, or `float('inf')`) when the accepted-change count is zero, and add a unit test asserting the degenerate case is handled gracefully.
evidence: 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes'
Tests section only specifies '3 attempts with 1 accepted returns summed llm_calls / 1'
ABSENCE: no mention of guard for zero-denominator case
findings.finding: cost-per-accepted-change divides total llm_calls by the count of PASS closes, but the plan specifies no handling for the zero-accepted case (ZeroDivisionError). Tests only exercise the happy path (1 PASS); no fixture or assertion covers a dataset with zero accepted changes.
findings.location: Approach section / AC line: 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes'
scenarios: A ticket whose sidecar contains only ERROR or FAIL rows yields zero PASS closes, causing an unhandled ZeroDivisionError at metric read time, crashing the metrics collection pipeline.
findings.suggested_fix: In `sidecar_metrics.py`, guard the division: `return total_llm_calls / accepted if accepted else None`. Add a test fixture with 3 FAIL rows and assert the result is `None` (or the chosen sentinel).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the access mechanism for all COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS records in the Approach (e.g., add `all_pass_records()` to `completion_sidecar.py` mirroring `all_review_results()`, or specify raw file enumeration) since no multi-record PASS reader currently exists.
evidence: completion_sidecar.py exposes only `latest_pass_record()` and `latest_fail_verdict()`, both single-record readers
plan_review/sidecar.py exposes `all_review_results()` as the multi-record analogue — no equivalent exists for COMPLETION_VERDICT
Plan: 'Read-only; nothing written' — but does not name which API enumerates all PASS records
findings.finding: For the cost-per-accepted-change metric the plan needs to walk all COMPLETION_VERDICT PASS records per ticket, but `completion_sidecar.py` only exposes `latest_pass_record()` (single most-recent). There is no `all_pass_records()` equivalent. The plan says this is a pure 'read-side derivation' but does not name the missing multi-record access path.
findings.location: Approach — 'cost-per-accepted-change divides summed llm_calls by accepted (PASS) closes'
scenarios: Implementation must either duplicate the raw file-walk from completion_sidecar.py or add a new all_pass_records() helper; without an explicit call-out, the implementer may attempt to use latest_pass_record() and produce a metric based on only the single most-recent PASS, silently undercounting.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `all_pass_records(ticket_id, ...)` to `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py` mirroring `all_review_results()` in `plan_review/sidecar.py`, and add `completion_sidecar.py` to the Files list as a modified target.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Approach how `sidecar_metrics.py` recovers the ticket association for `gate_error_v1` records (e.g. read the event envelope's routing key, or infer from the filesystem directory name), since the payload body has no `ticket_id` field.
evidence: gate_error_sidecar.py build_gate_error_payload returns {schema, verdict, gate, error} — no ticket_id key in the payload body
ticket_id is only the event-stream routing key passed to append_event(), stored in the event envelope, not the payload
Plan claims pairing by ticket without specifying whether it reads the envelope wrapper or the filesystem directory path to obtain ticket context
findings.finding: The `gate_error_v1` payload written by `gate_error_sidecar.build_gate_error_payload()` does not include a `ticket_id` field in the payload body — only `schema`, `verdict`, `gate`, and `error`. The plan's env-diagnosis derivation 'pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket', but the mechanism for recovering ticket association from an ERROR record's payload is unspecified and non-obvious.
findings.location: Approach section ('env-diagnosis pairs each ERROR with the next PASS on the same ticket') / src/rebar/llm/gate_error_sidecar.py:build_gate_error_payload()
scenarios: sidecar_metrics.py reads the payload body looking for ticket_id and gets None, silently drops all ERROR records from the pairing, yielding empty env-diagnosis intervals
Reader relies on directory path inference which may differ from envelope parsing used by other readers, creating inconsistency
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence: 'gate_error_v1 payloads lack a ticket_id field; the reader recovers ticket context from the event envelope (the `ticket_id` field written by `append_event`) or from the parent directory name in the tracker store, consistent with how `prune()` resolves ticket dirs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the leaf's Approach and at least one AC that each derived metric record emitted by `sidecar_metrics.py` carries `source` and `confidence` fields consistent with the parent's label contract (or explicitly reference the sibling ticket that owns the wrapping contract and confirm the leaf's output is wrapped there before registration).
evidence: Parent AC (86ac): 'Every metric entry carries source and confidence, and a source=backfill_classified value is never aggregated into an authoritative structural series — a unit test asserts is_authoritative("backfill_classified") is False and the segregation holds.'
Leaf Approach section: no mention of source or confidence fields on derived metrics.
Leaf Tests section: fixture-based assertions cover only numeric values (cost-per-accepted-change == 12, duration == delta); no assertion that emitted metric records carry source/confidence.
Leaf AC-1 and AC-2: assert only numeric correctness, not label compliance.

Parent Decomposition separates 'metric-registry + unavailable/label contract' as its own bullet, but the leaf neither declares a dependency on that sibling nor states its output conforms to the label contract.
findings.finding: The parent's AC requires every registered metric entry to carry `source` and `confidence` fields (and segregates `source=backfill_classified` from authoritative series), but the leaf's plan — including its Approach, Tests, and both ACs — is entirely silent on emitting or verifying these fields for the metrics it registers.
findings.location: Leaf AC section (both ACs) vs. Parent AC: 'Every metric entry carries source and confidence, and a source=backfill_classified value is never aggregated into an authoritative structural series'
scenarios: The leaf ships metrics registered without source/confidence fields; the parent's integration test ('every registered metric key is present with either a value or {"unavailable":…}') or the source/confidence unit test fails at integration time.
A future aggregation step treats the leaf's unlabeled structural-read metrics as backfill_classified (or vice versa) because the contract was never pinned in the leaf's plan.
findings.suggested_fix: In the leaf's Approach, add one sentence: 'Each derived metric is wrapped with source="structural" and confidence="authoritative" (the label-contract ticket owns the wrapper; this reader's output is passed through it before registration).' Add a third AC: 'Each metric record returned by the reader carries source and confidence fields — a unit test asserts their presence on the fixture output.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `tests/unit/metrics/test_registry.py` and `tests/unit/metrics/test_registry_heldout.py` as consumers of REGISTRY that will exercise every new MetricSpec added there; confirm the new specs' `source`, `confidence`, and `id` values satisfy the closed-vocabulary and uniqueness invariants those tests enforce.
evidence: test_registry.py: `ids = [spec.id for spec in REGISTRY]` + uniqueness assert
test_registry_heldout.py: `for spec in REGISTRY: assert spec.source in _SOURCES`
Plan names only sidecar_metrics.py and registry.py as targets; existing registry tests are not mentioned
findings.finding: The plan modifies `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py` (adds new MetricSpec entries to REGISTRY) but does not enumerate two existing external test consumers — `tests/unit/metrics/test_registry.py` and `tests/unit/metrics/test_registry_heldout.py` — which iterate over every spec in REGISTRY and assert id uniqueness plus closed source/confidence vocabularies. Any new specs added must satisfy those invariants or those tests will fail.
findings.location: Files section / Approach — 'registered in src/rebar/metrics/registry.py'
scenarios: A new MetricSpec registered with source='sidecar' (correct) but a duplicate id, or a source/confidence outside the closed vocabulary, would cause test_registry / test_registry_heldout to fail — silently breaking previously-passing tests.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the plan: 'New MetricSpec entries registered in REGISTRY must use source=\"sidecar\", confidence=\"high\", and unique ids; `test_registry.py` and `test_registry_heldout.py` will automatically validate them.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge and mitigate the implicit schema coupling between `sidecar_metrics.py` and the sidecar writer: either document the expected field contract (e.g., a `SidecarSchema` dataclass/TypedDict shared by both sides), add a schema-version guard in the reader, or note the dependency in AGENTS.md/a design doc so future schema changes know to update the reader.
evidence: Plan states the reader consumes 'per-run metrics{llm_calls,det_ms,llm_ms,total_ms}, finding decisions, and pass/fail verdicts' from sidecars written by a separate subsystem
No mention of a schema contract, version field check, or interface boundary between the sidecar writer and the new reader
'Read-only; nothing written' confirms one-way coupling with no acknowledgment of the dependency direction
findings.finding: The new sidecar reader implicitly couples to the sidecar schema (fields: llm_calls, det_ms, llm_ms, total_ms, verdict strings, ERROR outcome) written by the gate/verdict subsystem, but this cross-component dependency is not acknowledged, justified, or protected by an interface contract. A field rename or schema evolution in the sidecar writer will silently break the metrics reader with no guard at the boundary.
findings.location: Files section / Approach section — `src/rebar/metrics/sidecar_metrics.py`
scenarios: A future change renames `llm_calls` to `llm_call_count` in the sidecar writer; the metrics reader silently returns 0 or raises a KeyError with no schema-version guard
The verdict string format changes (e.g., 'PASS' -> 'pass'); the PASS-counting logic breaks without notice
findings.suggested_fix: Define a `SidecarRecord` TypedDict (or dataclass) in a shared location (e.g., `src/rebar/metrics/sidecar_schema.py`) listing the expected fields and their types; import it in both the writer and `sidecar_metrics.py`. Add a note in AGENTS.md that schema changes to sidecars require updating `sidecar_schema.py` and re-running the metrics unit tests.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify try/except error handling in `sidecar_metrics.py` for `FileNotFoundError` and parse errors (skip/log the offending file), and add a test asserting the reader returns an empty/partial result rather than raising when a sidecar is absent.
evidence: 'Read-only; nothing written.'
ABSENCE: no mention of try/except, default return, or logging for FileNotFoundError or JSON/parse errors
Approach says sidecars 'already persist' data but does not address the case where they are absent or truncated
findings.finding: The plan specifies no error handling for missing or malformed sidecar files, which are real file-I/O failure points (sidecars may not yet exist for a ticket, or may be partially written by a concurrent run).
findings.location: Approach section / Files: src/rebar/metrics/sidecar_metrics.py
scenarios: A sidecar file for a ticket is absent (first run, or file deleted) — reader raises FileNotFoundError uncaught.
A sidecar file is partially written (concurrent process still writing) — JSON parse error propagates uncaught, failing the entire metrics read.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap per-file reads in `try/except (FileNotFoundError, json.JSONDecodeError) as e: log.warning(...)` and continue to the next file; emit a metric counter for skipped files so operators can detect data gaps.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
